scenario: Kestrel Marketplace ships 4500 orders per day through a Camunda 8.8 inclusive gateway with three outgoing flows for pasta, steak, and salad. The XML must declare a default outgoing sequence flow so an instance never raises an incident when no condition matches.
question: How is the default outgoing flow declared in the XML for an inclusive gateway?

options.text: The bpmn:inclusiveGateway carries a defaultFlow child element whose value is the name of the outgoing flow taken when every condition evaluates to false
The bpmn:inclusiveGateway carries a fallbackRef attribute whose value is the id of a catching event that fires when each outgoing condition evaluates to false
The bpmn:sequenceFlow chosen as default carries an extensionElement that flags it as default so the engine picks it instead of raising an incident later
The bpmn:inclusiveGateway carries a default attribute whose value is the id of the outgoing sequence flow taken when no outgoing condition is true

a.text: Incorrect. The documented XML uses a default attribute on the gateway element, not a defaultFlow child element, and the value is a sequence flow id rather than a flow name.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no fallbackRef attribute on the inclusive gateway; the documented mechanism for fallback routing is the gateway's default attribute pointing at one sequence flow id.

c.text: Incorrect. The default flow is declared on the gateway itself through the default attribute and not through an extensionElement on the sequence flow element.

d.text: Correct. The documented XML shows bpmn:inclusiveGateway with a default attribute whose value is the id of the outgoing sequence flow taken when no other outgoing condition evaluates to true.

explanation: The documented XML representation places a default attribute on the bpmn:inclusiveGateway element whose value is the id of the outgoing sequence flow taken when none of the other outgoing conditions evaluates to true.
